front: Short Story: people climb ladders to collect milk from the moon; the narrator's Deaf cousin is best at it; Mrs. Vhd Vhd gets trapped on the moon and stays for love of the Deaf One
back: "The Distance of the Moon" (Italo Calvino, from Cosmicomics)

front: Short Story: the world gains color for the first time; Qfwfq's love interest Ayl retreats into the earth
Short Story: life was "packed like sardines" in a singularity; the Big Bang happens when Mrs. Ph(i)Nk0 offers to make noodles for everyone
back: "All at One Point" (Italo Calvino, from Cosmicomics)

front: Short Story: a hunter frozen in the moment his arrow flies toward a pouncing lion contemplates possible pasts and futures
Short Story: the narrator responds "WHAT OF IT?" to a sign saying "I SAW YOU" and creates signs for good and bad actions
Short Story: the narrator and Lieutenant Fenimore both love Ursula as they travel on parallel paths through space that cannot cross
back: "The Form of Space" (Italo Calvino, from Cosmicomics)

front: Novel: opens "You are about to begin reading..."; you, the Reader, encounter Ludmilla at a bookstore; odd-numbered chapters in second person
back: If on a winter's night a traveler (Italo Calvino)
work: If on a winter's night a traveler

front: Novel: the writer Silas Flannery is translated by the chaos-inducing Ermes Marana; features the Organization of Apocryphal Power
back: If on a winter's night a traveler (Italo Calvino)
work: If on a winter's night a traveler

front: Novel: features the fictional countries Cimmeria (near Gulf of Bothnia) and the Cimbrian People's Republic; Professor Uzzi-Tuzii teaches Cimmerian literature
back: If on a winter's night a traveler (Italo Calvino)
work: If on a winter's night a traveler

front: Novel: framed as conversations between Marco Polo and Kublai Khan; Polo describes 55 cities named after women, including Isidora, Diomira, and Ersilia
Novel: cities are "like dreams, made of desires and fears"; Khan wonders if his empire is "nothing but a zodiac of the mind's phantasm" while playing chess
Novel: Cosimo refuses to eat snails prepared by his sister Battista and flees to live in the trees; hunts with dachshund Ottimo Massimo
back: The Baron in the Trees (Italo Calvino)

front: Novel: Medardo of Terralba is split into good and evil halves by a cannonball; the first of the Our Ancestors trilogy
Novel: the crusader Agilulf is only a set of floating armor serving as Charlemagne's paladin alongside Rambaldo
Essay: Harvard lectures on lightness, quickness, exactitude, visibility, and multiplicity; "Lightness" tells of Cavalcanti leaping over his foes
back: Six Memos for the Next Millennium (Italo Calvino)
work: Six Memos for the Next Millennium / Why Read the Classics?

front: Novel: the title character tries to "see" a wave from a beach; divided into 3x3x3 chapters; realizes his front lawn is infinite
Novel: characters who cannot speak communicate using tarot cards; includes characters like Roland and the Alchemist
back: The Castle of Crossed Destinies (Italo Calvino)

front: Short Story: Olivia obsesses over Aztec human sacrifice; represents the sense of taste in an unfinished five-senses collection
